name: El Ronquillo Greenway
name_original: Vía Verde del Ronquillo — ida y vuelta por el Embalse de la Minilla
description: Hike the El Ronquillo Greenway, a 7.1-mile (11.5 km) moderate route along the La Minilla Reservoir, offering scenic views and easy access.
region_intro: Hiking around El Ronquillo offers access to diverse natural features within the Sierra Norte of Seville, bordering the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The landscape is characterized by the Rivera de Huelva and Rivera del Cala rivers, which create scenic areas like Los Cachones. A central feature is the La Minilla Reservoir, with its surrounding greenway. The region also includes forests of the Sierra de Bermeja, featuring oak trees and rural paths, alongside dehesas of oak and cork oak. Best hiking trails around El Ronquillo The most popular hiking route is El Ronquillo Greenway, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes about 3 hours to complete. This route follows a converted old railway line along the La Minilla Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Path Between Stone Walls – Scenic Rocky Path loop from Las Cañadillas, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. This trail winds through forests of the Sierra de Bermeja, notable for its dry stone walls. Local hikers also love the Castillo de las Guardas – El Castillo de las Guardas loop from Bar Casa Juanito, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail leading through rolling hills and Mediterranean forests, often completed in about 4 hours. Hiking around El Ronquillo is defined by river valleys, reservoir paths, and dehesas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate treks. The routes in El Ronquillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore El Ronquillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around El Ronquillo?
A: There are currently &lt;strong&gt;7 hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; documented around El Ronquillo, offering a range of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging moderate treks. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in El Ronquillo?
A: Hiking in El Ronquillo features diverse terrain, including paths along river valleys like the Rivera de Huelva and Rivera del Cala, routes around the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and trails through Mediterranean forests known as dehesas. You'll also find rural paths winding through the Sierra de Bermeja, often bordered by ancient dry stone walls.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or a relaxed walk?
A: Yes, for an easier experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38140809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir (south) loop from El Menor&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 5.8 km long and is classified as easy, making it perfect for a relaxed walk with scenic views of the reservoir.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can enjoy several natural features and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a central highlight, with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010352668/el-ronquillo-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Ronquillo Greenway&lt;/a&gt; running along its banks. You'll also encounter the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5084729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Huelva&lt;/a&gt;, and the distinctive dry stone walls along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5249066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Between Stone Walls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes in El Ronquillo are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Between Stone Walls – Scenic Rocky Path loop from Las Cañadillas&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 14.2 km loop through forests. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21846020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Guardas – El Castillo de las Guardas loop from Bar Casa Juanito&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 8.9-mile trail through rolling hills.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Ronquillo?
A: El Ronquillo is part of the Sierra Norte of Seville, which generally experiences mild winters and hot summers. Spring and autumn are typically the most pleasant seasons for hiking, offering comfortable temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Winter can also be suitable, especially for longer treks, while summer might be best for early morning or late afternoon walks to avoid the heat.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in El Ronquillo?
A: For families, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010352668/el-ronquillo-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Ronquillo Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 11.5 km converted railway line offers a relatively flat and accessible path along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, suitable for various ages and even cycling. The easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38140809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir (south) loop from El Menor&lt;/a&gt; is also a good option for a shorter, gentle walk.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in El Ronquillo?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, many natural areas in Spain, including those in the Sierra Norte, generally welcome dogs on trails, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always advisable to keep your dog under control and clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the area offers options for refreshments. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cantina&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known traditional stopping point for hikers, offering a place to rest and enjoy a drink. The village of El Ronquillo itself also provides various establishments.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in El Ronquillo?
A: The hiking routes around El Ronquillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene reservoir paths to the unique dehesas and forests with their ancient dry stone walls. The variety of routes catering to different ability levels is also frequently appreciated.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the hiking trailheads?
A: El Ronquillo is a small town, and while there might be limited bus services connecting it to larger towns in Seville, direct public transport to specific trailheads outside the village is generally not extensive. It's often recommended to use a car to access the starting points of many routes, especially those further afield.

Q: Are there any specific wildlife I might encounter while hiking?
A: Being part of or bordering the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, the region is rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, including the impressive griffon vulture. The dehesas, with their oak and cork oak trees, provide a habitat for diverse fauna, notably the Iberian pig, which can sometimes be seen grazing.

Q: What is the longest moderate hike available in El Ronquillo?
A: The longest moderate hikes documented are the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Between Stone Walls – Scenic Rocky Path loop from Las Cañadillas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21846020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Guardas – El Castillo de las Guardas loop from Bar Casa Juanito&lt;/a&gt;, both around 14.2-14.3 km (8.8-8.9 miles) in length and typically taking about 4 hours to complete.
tips: The La Minilla Reservoir belongs to the basin of the Guadalquivir Hydrographic Confederation and is located in the province of Seville. Its construction was begun by the Seville City Council in 1942, although it was completed by the Guadalquivir Hydrographic Confederation.

At the southern end of the Minilla Reservoir there is a rest area with a restaurant and close to the road that comes from Ronquillo. The dam wall is not at this point but there are some very good views of the dam.

faqs.question: Is the El Ronquillo Greenway suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered to have a 'Low' difficulty level, making it suitable for all skill levels, including families and casual walkers. While there are a couple of short, steep sections, the overall compacted earth path ensures a comfortable experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the El Ronquillo Greenway?
faqs.answer: The trail's surface is primarily compacted earth, typical of converted railway lines. You'll traverse a landscape of pasture land and oak woods, interspersed with Mediterranean forest and scrubland. There are two points with steep slopes, but the path is generally accessible.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers magnificent natural scenery, with the La Minilla Reservoir consistently in view. You'll also pass through picturesque Mediterranean forest landscapes and open stretches where you can observe livestock. A notable feature is the restored old railway station, which now serves as a hotel-restaurant and active tourism center.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the El Ronquillo Greenway?
faqs.answer: Parking facilities are available along the route, particularly near recreational areas. The restored old railway station also offers amenities and is a good starting point with parking.

faqs.question: Is the El Ronquillo Greenway dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Greenways are generally accessible for dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect local wildlife and other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy the El Ronquillo Greenway.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the El Ronquillo Greenway?
faqs.answer: Given the Mediterranean climate and the presence of open sections, spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and some sections may offer less shade.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route follows the Vía Verde de El Ronquillo (100%) and also aligns with sections of the Vía Verde de El Ronquillo (Ant. Fer.: M. Cala - S. Juan Az.) (100%).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services available along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the restored old railway station along the route now functions as a hotel-restaurant and an active tourism center, offering amenities and local cuisine. Several recreational areas are also available.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: Along the trail, you might hear the cry of an azure-winged magpie or observe livestock in the more open stretches of pasture land. The reservoir also suggests the presence of fish and other aquatic life.

faqs.question: Is there an option for a longer adventure beyond the main greenway?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route of the old railway extends beyond the developed greenway towards the Minas de Cala mines. This extended section is marked but not officially developed as a greenway, offering a more rugged experience for those seeking a longer adventure.
